name: The Ultimate Guide to Appealing a Denied Medical Claim in Utah and Idaho
description: The Ultimate Guide to Appealing a Denied Medical Claim in Utah and Idaho - Utah County

step.name: Step 1
step.text: Receiving a denial letter from a workers' compensation insurer can feel like a devastating blow, but in the legal world, it is often just the opening move. Insurance companies frequently deny expensive medical treatments—like spinal surgeries, MRIs, or long-term physical therapy—hoping that the injured worker will simply give up. Under the laws of Utah, Idaho, and Colorado, you have the right to challenge these decisions.

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Every formal denial must come in writing and list a specific reason. Common reasons include 'lack of medical necessity,' 'pre-existing condition,' or 'injury not work-related.' Understanding the 'why' is crucial for building your appeal.

step.name: Step 5
step.text: Share the denial letter with your treating physician. Often, a 'Letter of Medical Necessity' from your doctor can overturn an adjuster's denial. If your doctor can explain specifically why a surgery is required due to the work accident (and not just age), it creates powerful evidence for your case.

step.name: Step 6
step.text: ### Step 3: File an Application for Hearing

step.name: Step 7
step.text: In Utah, this is a formal legal document filed with the Labor Commission. It moves your case from the insurance company's desk to a judge's desk. Once this is filed, strict discovery rules apply, and the insurance company can no longer just ignore your requests.

step.name: Step 8
step.text: ### Step 4: Gather Evidence and Expert Testimony

step.name: Step 9
step.text: To win an appeal, you need more than just a 'feeling' that the treatment is necessary. You may need an Independent Medical Examination (IME) from a third-party doctor who isn't on the insurance company's payroll. You will also need to gather all past medical records to prove that your current condition was caused by the work event.

step.name: Step 11
step.text: Many cases are settled in mediation before they ever reach a judge. A mediator helps both sides reach an agreement—usually, the insurance company agrees to pay for the treatment in exchange for a structured plan. If mediation fails, your attorney will represent you in a formal hearing, presenting evidence and cross-examining witnesses.

step.name: Step 13
step.text: The appeal process is highly technical, with strict deadlines and evidentiary rules. At Just Work Comp Law, attorneys like Halston Davis and Mark Sanchez have navigated thousands of these appeals. We know the tactics insurers use and how to defeat them. If your medical treatment has been denied, don't wait for your health to get worse—contact us today.
